description: Improve MCP tool descriptions and Zod schema consistency to provide sufficient context for MCP clients. Covers browser tools (composability hints, return value documentation), Slack plugin tools (description clarity, composability guidance), and parameter naming normalization.

userStories.title: Improve browser tool descriptions with composability hints
userStories.description: As an MCP client, I want browser tool descriptions to explain what they return and how to discover tab IDs so that I can chain browser tool operations without guessing.
acceptanceCriteria: browser_list_tabs description mentions that returned tab IDs can be used with browser_close_tab, browser_navigate_tab, and browser_execute_script
browser_open_tab description mentions returning a tab ID and references other browser tools by name
browser_close_tab description references browser_list_tabs for tab ID discovery
browser_navigate_tab description references browser_list_tabs for tab ID discovery
userStories.notes:
Edit 4 files in platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/:

1. list-tabs.ts line 11: Change description to 'List all open browser tabs. Returns tab ID, title, URL, and active status for each tab. Use the returned tab IDs with browser_close_tab, browser_navigate_tab, and browser_execute_script.'

2. open-tab.ts line 12: Change description to 'Open a new browser tab with the specified URL. Returns the new tab ID, which can be used with browser_navigate_tab, browser_close_tab, and browser_execute_script.'

3. close-tab.ts line 11: Change description to 'Close a browser tab by its tab ID. Use browser_list_tabs to find tab IDs.'

4. navigate-tab.ts line 11: Change description to 'Navigate an existing browser tab to a new URL. Use browser_list_tabs to find tab IDs.'

userStories.title: Add JSON-serializability note to browser_execute_script description
userStories.description: As an MCP client, I want browser_execute_script to document that only JSON-serializable values can be returned so that I don't write code returning DOM nodes or functions and get confusing empty results.
acceptanceCriteria: browser_execute_script description includes a note about JSON-serializability constraint
Description mentions strings, numbers, booleans, arrays, plain objects as valid return types
Description mentions DOM nodes, functions, and circular references as non-returnable
userStories.notes:
Edit platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/execute-script.ts lines 21-26. Append to the existing description string (which is already multi-line concatenated with +):

'The return value must be JSON-serializable (strings, numbers, booleans, arrays, plain objects). DOM nodes, functions, and circular references cannot be returned.'

The existing description already ends with example code snippets. Add the serializability note after the examples line.

userStories.title: Improve read_thread and list_members Slack tool descriptions
userStories.description: As an MCP client, I want Slack tool descriptions to describe behavior (not internal APIs) and guide me toward composable multi-tool workflows so that I can use the tools effectively.
acceptanceCriteria: read_thread description no longer mentions 'conversations.replies API'
read_thread description mentions that it returns replies including the parent message
list_members description mentions 'user IDs only' and references get_user_profile and list_users for resolving IDs to names
cd plugins/slack && bun run build succeeds
userStories.notes:
Edit 2 files in plugins/slack/src/tools/:

1. read-thread.ts line 15: Change description from 'Read replies in a Slack thread using conversations.replies API' to 'Read replies in a Slack thread. Returns all messages including the parent message, with optional pagination.'

2. list-members.ts line 7: Change description from 'List members of a Slack channel with optional pagination' to 'List member user IDs of a Slack channel with optional pagination. Returns user IDs only — use get_user_profile to resolve a specific user ID to a name/profile, or list_users to get all workspace users with names.'

After editing, rebuild the plugin: cd plugins/slack && bun run build

userStories.title: Normalize message timestamp parameter name to ts across all Slack tools
userStories.description: As an MCP client, I want all Slack tools that accept a message timestamp to use the same parameter name (ts) so that I don't have to check each tool's schema for whether it's called ts or timestamp.
acceptanceCriteria: pin_message input schema uses parameter name 'ts' instead of 'timestamp'
unpin_message input schema uses parameter name 'ts' instead of 'timestamp'
add_reaction input schema uses parameter name 'ts' instead of 'timestamp'
remove_reaction input schema uses parameter name 'ts' instead of 'timestamp'
Each tool's ts parameter has a description matching the pattern used by other tools: 'Timestamp of the message to [action] — serves as the unique message ID (e.g., 1234567890.123456)'
Each tool handler maps params.ts to the Slack API's expected 'timestamp' field in the slackApi call
Generated opentabs-plugin.json reflects the parameter name change
cd plugins/slack && bun run build succeeds
userStories.notes:
Edit 4 files in plugins/slack/src/tools/. In each file:
1. Rename the Zod input parameter from 'timestamp' to 'ts'
2. Update the .describe() to match existing ts convention
3. Update the handler to map ts back to timestamp for slackApi

pin-message.ts:
- Input: rename timestamp → ts, description: 'Timestamp of the message to pin — serves as the unique message ID (e.g., 1234567890.123456)'
- Handler: change params.timestamp to params.ts, but pass { channel: params.channel, timestamp: params.ts } to slackApi (Slack API expects 'timestamp')

unpin-message.ts:
- Same pattern: rename timestamp → ts in schema, map ts → timestamp in handler
- Description: 'Timestamp of the message to unpin — serves as the unique message ID (e.g., 1234567890.123456)'

add-reaction.ts:
- Same pattern: rename timestamp → ts in schema, map ts → timestamp in handler
- Description: 'Timestamp of the message to react to — serves as the unique message ID (e.g., 1234567890.123456)'

remove-reaction.ts:
- Same pattern: rename timestamp → ts in schema, map ts → timestamp in handler
- Description: 'Timestamp of the message to remove the reaction from — serves as the unique message ID (e.g., 1234567890.123456)'

After editing all 4 files, rebuild: cd plugins/slack && bun run build
Verify the generated opentabs-plugin.json shows 'ts' not 'timestamp' for these tools.
